body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies a documentation gap in the provisioning generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning`) — specifically, the `ResourceVersions` behavior around conditional preview API version filtering is not reflected in `provisioning-library-spec.md` or `design.md`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (provisioning generator in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`)
**Affected API:** `ResourceVersions` nested class / `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`
**Scenarios:**
- When the default API version is GA, only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews excluded)
- When the default API version is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are listed
- The `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic (strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`) is undocumented

**Root ask:** Update `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` and `docs/design.md` to document the conditional logic governing which API versions are included in `ResourceVersions`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The behavior change was introduced in commit 21456f8 (PR #57862). The generated test files `ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs` and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` already reflect the new behavior; only the documentation lags behind.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs` to confirm the `IsPreviewApiVersion` logic and how `ResourceVersions` is populated.
2. Update the "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section in `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` to describe both the GA-default and preview-default behaviors.
3. Update Section 6 "Output Types" in `docs/design.md` to reflect the conditional version inclusion logic.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — The issue is about the provisioning generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning`) which generates `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries.
- **Service:** `CodeGen` — The affected files are under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`, which is a code generator package per the label rules.
- **Confidence:** High — Identical label combination (`Provisioning` + `CodeGen`) confirmed by multiple similar reference issues: #57489, #57488, #57165, all filed against the provisioning generator in `eng/packages/`.
</details>
